title: Improve Scorecard History stakeholder summary
description: Revise Scorecard History generated content so the top-level report gives a concise stakeholder/SME-facing bullet summary of high-level guideline and code/prompt changes in plain language, prioritizing rubric/guideline meaning over technical implementation detail.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Retuned the Scorecard History LLM summary prompt toward stakeholder/SME meeting language: high-level what changed, guideline/rubric meaning first, code/prompt-only changes translated into scoring behavior, and no version IDs or implementation terminology.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Follow-up from manual report run: the stakeholder summary tone improved, but the LLM incorrectly claimed all changes were promoted. Added aggregate counts and champion_coverage to the prompt payload and instructed the LLM to use that exact value for rollout coverage.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Second manual run exposed guideline-count drift: the LLM said no rubric wording changed despite guideline diffs. Added aggregate and per-score guideline/code change counts to the prompt payload and explicit instructions not to claim no rubric wording changed unless the aggregate guideline count is zero.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: New requirement: add SME/stakeholder rubric-clarification questions after the high-level change summary. Prefer questions found in recent procedures associated with included score versions; when unavailable, derive concise SME questions from RCA/misclassification context during report generation.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Added SME question sourcing to Scorecard History: version-level Procedure lookup, score-level recent Procedure lookup, extraction of optimizer SME agenda/worksheet fields, and S3-backed procedure state support when dashboard_state/state is offloaded. The top summary now requires a Questions for SMEs / stakeholders section and can derive questions from RCA context when procedure agendas are unavailable.
